title: Dark Reaper
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Citation}} {{Weapon |image=[[File:DarkReaper.jpg]] |name=Dark Reaper |manufacturer= |model= |type=[[Superweapon/Legends|Superweapon]] |culture= |creators=[[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]][[Star Wars: The Official Starships & Vehicles Collection 78|''Star Wars: The Official Starships & Vehicles Collection'' 78]] |created=Around [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]]''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' |destroyed=[[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] |discovered=*[[3996 BBY]] *[[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] |owners= |locations= |cost= |value= |shape= |size= |material= |length= |width=Approx. 100 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] |diam= |height=Approx. 60 meters |weight= |protection= |crew= |capacity= |range= |inscription= |markings= |purpose= |heritage= |affiliation=*[[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]] *[[Brotherhood of the Sith]] *[[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] }} {{Quote|During the Great Sith War, our enemies constructed a weapon so powerful, no army could stand against it. It was known as… the Dark Reaper.|[[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]]|[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars (video game)|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'' video game]]}} The '''Dark Reaper''' was a [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] created during the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]] ([[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]]). ==Description== {{Quote|The Reaper required tremendous amounts of energy to function, so the Sith crafted a device that harvested the living force to provide its power.|[[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]]|}} The Dark Reaper worked by harvesting [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] using the [[Force Harvester]], a device that killed anything within range by draining its Force energy and focusing it into beams that dealt massive damage to any object. However, the Reaper could not harvest a wound in the Force. The Reaper was also equipped with [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]]-powered [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]]. In order to defeat the Dark Reaper, an ancient [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] named [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] taught the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] how to withstand the Dark Reaper's effects. ==History== ===Great Sith War=== {{Main|Dark Reaper campaign}} {{Quote|How was the Reaper defeated?''"
"''A fallen Jedi Knight named Ulic Qel-Droma taught the Jedi how to withstand the Reaper's effects. After the war, all information about the Reaper, including Ulic's secrets, were lost. The Jedi scattered the remaining pieces to the far corners of the galaxy.|[[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]]|}} A weapon of mass destruction, it was created by the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] in an [[Armory (Thule)|armory]] on [[Thule/Legends|Thule]]''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]'' during the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]] and used against the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. During the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] rediscovered it doing excavations on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. As it needed massive amounts of energy to function, the [[Krath/Legends|Krath]] had it target the [[Living Force/Legends|Living Force]] to provide power. When fallen Jedi [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] returned from the Krath he told the Jedi how to defeat the Reaper. The [[Dark Reaper campaign|final]] battle was fought on the world of [[Thule/Legends|Thule]], deep within the territory of the old Sith Empire. In a calculated strike, a Jedi task force staged an attack on the Dark Reaper and using Ulic's information, were able to disable the weapon. Breaking the deadly device apart, the Republic made great effort to hide the pieces on a myriad of different [[Planet/Legends|planets]]. They then hid the power source of the Dark Reaper, the [[Force Harvester]], on the junkyard world of [[Raxus Prime/Legends|Raxus Prime]]. During the [[Great Galactic War]], the [[Tenebrae|Sith Emperor]] and [[Darth Malgus]] thought the Reaper was buried on [[Ord Radama/Legends|Ord Radama]].''[[Book of Sith: Secrets from the Dark Side]]'' ===Clone Wars=== {{Main|Dark Reaper crisis}} {{Quote|Ulic Qel-Droma, you defeated the Dark Reaper during the Sith War. Show me how to stop it again.''"
"''Yes, I can teach you to harness the power of the Force around you, making you immune to the Dark Reaper's effects, for a short time. But this knowledge comes at great risk.|[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] and [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma's]] [[Force ghost/Legends|spirit]]|}} [[File:Ulics_Tomb.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Anakin Skywalker enters Ulic Qel-Droma's tomb to learn how to stop the Dark Reaper.]] Four thousand years later, during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] and his [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] [[Cydon Prax]] scoured [[Raxus Prime/Legends|Raxus Prime]] for the [[Force Harvester]]. It was a vital part in reconstructing the Dark Reaper to destroy the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. He used Cydon Prax to activate the Force Harvester, destroying the forest on a [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]] [[Alaris Prime/Legends|moon]] as a test of its power. To test its power, he planned to use it on captured Wookiees, [[Bera Kazan]], and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], whom he'd captured on [[Raxus Prime/Legends|Raxus Prime]]. The wave of energy given off killed half of the thriving forest on the moon. Anakin and Bera barely escaped the wave, and then contacted the Republic to warn them of what Dooku had uncovered. Dooku then used the Harvester to collect energy on [[Dac/Legends|Mon Calamari]], [[Agamar/Legends|Agamar]] and [[Bakura/Legends|Bakura]]. The death of millions in the [[Attack on Mon Calamari (Clone Wars)|attack]] on Mon Calamari caused the planet's inhabitants to cooperate with the Confederacy, including [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Merai]]. [[File:Dark_Reaper_NEC.jpg|thumb|80px|Dark Reaper]] Dooku searched [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] and found the pieces of the Dark Reaper. The exact locations were unknown, but [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] stated that it was separated into the farthest reaches of the galaxy, making [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] systems likely choices. Dooku then went to [[Thule/Legends|Thule]] and activated the Dark Reaper in an ancient [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] city on the planet. On [[Rhen Var/Legends|Rhen Var]], Anakin Skywalker went to the tomb of Ulic Qel-Droma for information on how to withstand the effects of Dark Reaper and the Harvester. Shortly after the Dark Reaper was revived, Anakin led an attack on the city and destroyed the Reaper using his [[TX-130 Saber-class fighter tank/Legends|TX-130 fighter tank]], disobeying an order from [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] to wait for backup. Although Anakin did manage to destroy the Reaper, the knowledge he gained from Qel-Droma caused him to act more arrogantly towards Obi-Wan, and indirectly led him to take a step towards the path to the dark side of the Force. ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' {{Codex}} *[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars (video game)|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'' video game]] {{1st}} ==Sources== [[File:Dark_Reaper_Book_of_Siths.jpg|thumb|260px|The core mechanism of the Dark Reaper as recorded in the [[Qel-Droma Holocron]]]] {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of the Dark Reaper}} *[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] *{{InsiderCite|68|Comics Link}} *{{FactFile|104|SUR 1-2|The Survival of Boba Fett}} *{{FactFile|107|SUR 3-6|The Survival of Boba Fett}} *{{FactFile|109|DEF 1-4|Defence of Kamino}} *{{FactFile|116|DOO 5-8|Count Dooku}} *''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]'' *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' *[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] *''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' *''[[Book of Sith: Secrets from the Dark Side]]'' *''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' *{{FactFile2014|6|22 BBY 75-80|Dark Side Influence}} *{{FactFile2014|11|SKY 23-26|Anakin Skywalker}} *{{FactFile2014|15|RAX 1-4|Raxus Prime}} *{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-part-12-tales-of-the-new-republic|series=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|text=Part 12: Tales of the New Republic}} *{{FactFile2014|40|19 BBY 15-20|Coruscant politics}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Dunkler Verheerer#legends |es=Segadora Oscura }} [[Category:Sith weapons]] [[Category:Superweapons]]
